executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 367,581.78 inc VAT (338,366.78 ex VAT) for the week ending 13 June 2026.

Overall gross margin landed at 17.41%, which requires close scrutiny given the significant variance between reported department margins and the overall blended figure, suggesting non-fresh departments and stock movement distortions are weighing heavily on the result.
Total waste of 8,590.86 represents 2.54% of ex-VAT sales, which is above acceptable thresholds and is a primary driver of margin erosion this week.
Cigarettes returned a negative margin of -24.68% against a scan margin of 12.87%, indicating a significant stock valuation discrepancy or write-off event that must be investigated immediately.

Several fresh departments including Poultry, Fish, Produce, Deli, and Provisions are reporting cost of sales figures that are negative or near-zero, strongly suggesting stock counts, purchase postings, or closing stock figures require verification.
Produce waste at 2,289.59 is the single largest waste line, followed by Fish at 1,659.91 and Deli at 1,408.03, collectively accounting for 62% of total store waste.
The fresh and deli categories are showing inflated reported margins well above scan margin, which is a red flag for stock count accuracy rather than genuine trading outperformance.
biggest_wins: Beef delivered 28.66% margin on sales of 13,218.63, comfortably ahead of its scan margin of 19.57%, representing a solid trading performance in the strongest-volume fresh meat line.
Lamb achieved a 48.51% margin on sales of 1,562.31, well above its scan margin of 24.80%, indicating effective management of cuts and yield this week.

Deli recorded sales of 8,732.62 against a scan margin of 41.17%, and while the reported 100.58% margin reflects a stock count anomaly, the underlying department volume and scan margin position are strong.
Provisions generated 24,964.47 in sales, the second-highest sales line across the fresh block, with a scan margin of 19.98% suggesting healthy underlying trade.
Produce posted the highest sales volume in the fresh block at 39,662.70, maintaining strong customer footfall in a key destination category.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes reported a margin of -24.68% on sales of 7,137.91 with a cost of sales of 8,899.72. Opening stock was 123,650.74 and closing stock was 114,751.02, producing a COGS that exceeds sales by 1,761.81. This is the most urgent investigation priority - verify stock count accuracy, check for unposted deliveries, or identify if a write-off has been incorrectly applied.

Poultry is showing a negative cost of sales of -359.81, producing a reported margin of 102.83% on sales of 12,696.55. Closing stock of 2,313.43 exceeds opening stock of 1,953.62 with no purchase figure reconciling the movement. Stock count and purchase posting must be reviewed.

Fish reported a cost of sales of just 32.00 on sales of 5,393.66, producing a 99.41% margin versus a scan margin of 15.30%. Combined with waste of 1,659.91, this department's stock figures are almost certainly incorrect and require an urgent recount.

Produce waste of 2,289.59 on sales of 39,662.70 represents approximately 5.8% waste-to-sales within the category, which is significantly above target. Ordering volumes, display quantities, and markdown practices must be reviewed.

Deli waste of 1,408.03 is the third-highest waste line in the store. At a scan margin of 41.17%, every pound of waste in this department has a disproportionate margin impact and production scheduling needs review.
Provisions waste of 986.03 is notable and, alongside the negative cost of sales figure, suggests stock management and posting accuracy issues that compound the margin distortion.

Total store waste of 8,590.86 at 2.54% of sales is above acceptable levels. Fish, Produce, Deli, and Provisions alone account for 6,343.56 of this total, pointing to concentrated ordering and production control failures in these departments.

Bacon and Loose S.R.P. reported a 93.62% margin on sales of 1,052.06 against a scan margin of 11.76%. The cost of sales of 67.16 on a department with opening stock of 595.94 and closing stock of 528.78 does not credibly reflect normal trading and requires stock verification.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Urgent - margin of -24.68% on sales of 7,137.91. COGS of 8,899.72 exceeds sales, generating a loss of 1,761.81. Scan margin is 12.87%. Investigate stock count and purchase postings immediately.
Beef: Performing well at 28.66% margin on 13,218.63 sales, ahead of scan margin of 19.57%. Waste is contained at 89.06. No immediate concerns but maintain current management standards.
Lamb: Strong margin of 48.51% on 1,562.31 sales, nearly double the scan margin of 24.80%. Waste of 85.09 is low. Review yield reporting to ensure margin is genuinely achieved.

Pork: Reported margin of 91.61% on sales of 1,732.19 against a scan margin of 23.19%. Cost of sales of 145.41 on opening stock of 527.65 and closing stock of 382.24 appears understated. Recheck stock figures and purchase postings.

Bacon and Loose S.R.P.: Reported margin of 93.62% on 1,052.06 sales against a scan margin of 11.76%. Cost of sales of 67.16 does not align with stock movements. Waste of 156.27 is present. Stock count verification required.

Poultry: Negative cost of sales of -359.81 producing a reported margin of 102.83% on sales of 12,696.55. Waste of 291.06 is present. Closing stock exceeds opening stock with no reconciling purchases visible. Full department audit required.

Fish: Cost of sales of 32.00 on sales of 5,393.66 gives a 99.41% margin versus a scan margin of 15.30%. Waste is 1,659.91, the second-highest in the store. Stock count and purchase postings must be corrected before week close.

Produce: Highest sales in the fresh block at 39,662.70 and a reported margin of 96.29% against a scan margin of 19.16%. Waste of 2,289.59 is the store's largest single waste line. Ordering and markdown disciplines need immediate review.

Deli: Sales of 8,732.62 with a scan margin of 41.17%. Reported margin of 100.58% reflects negative cost of sales of -50.95, indicating a stock or posting issue. Waste of 1,408.03 is significant and production scheduling should be reviewed.

Provisions: Sales of 24,964.47 with negative cost of sales of -197.95 producing a reported margin of 100.79% versus scan margin of 19.98%. Waste of 986.03 is elevated. Purchase postings and stock figures require reconciliation.
action_plan: Conduct an immediate recount of the Cigarettes stock and reconcile all purchase invoices for the week. A loss of 1,761.81 in a low-margin category at -24.68% is the highest financial priority for investigation before any period close.

Review all purchase postings for Poultry, Fish, Pork, Bacon, Produce, Deli, and Provisions. Multiple departments are showing negative or near-zero cost of sales which is not credible and must be corrected to produce an accurate weekly P&L.

Carry out physical stock counts in Fish and Poultry this week to validate closing stock figures. These two departments alone show combined reported profit of 18,418.02 on figures that do not reconcile with scan margin, waste, or stock movements.

Implement a daily waste sign-off process in Produce. Waste of 2,289.59 equates to approximately 5.8% of Produce sales. The department manager must review ordering quantities, review markdown timing, and ensure all near-date product is actioned by close of trade each day.

Review Deli production schedules and batch quantities. Waste of 1,408.03 in a department with a 41.17% scan margin represents a direct and avoidable margin loss. Introduce production-to-demand sheets for the coming week.

Address Fish waste of 1,659.91 as a critical action. Review order frequency, display quantities, and whether markdown protocols are being followed. Consider whether an additional mid-week delivery schedule could reduce the need for large opening orders.

Schedule a Provisions waste review with the department manager. Waste of 986.03 alongside a distorted cost of sales figure means the true margin position of this department is unclear. Verify date rotation and ordering practices.

Investigate Bacon and Loose S.R.P. margin discrepancy. A reported margin of 93.62% against a scan margin of 11.76% on sales of 1,052.06 indicates a stock posting error. Ensure all deliveries are correctly receipted.

Produce a corrected department P&L once all stock counts and purchase postings are reconciled. The overall reported margin of 17.41% should be retested against corrected figures to give an accurate view of true trading performance for the week.

Brief department managers on total store waste of 8,590.86 at 2.54% of sales in the weekly trading meeting. Set a waste reduction target for the coming week and require each high-waste department to submit a corrective action by Monday.

Review Lamb margin of 48.51% against a scan margin of 24.80% to confirm whether the outperformance is driven by genuine yield management or by a stock count variance. If genuine, document the practice for replication.

Ensure all closing stock counts submitted for week ending 13 June 2026 are validated by a second counter in departments with material variances before the P&L is finalised and submitted to head office.
